name: uses slack formatting
response: Here are the main features of <http://shadow.ai|shadow.ai>:  *Core Capabilities:* • *Slack Messaging* — Responds in Slack threads, monitors channels • *MCP Tool Access* — Connects to external services (Jira, Azure DevOps, Sentry, Grafana, MongoDB, New Relic, GCP logging, Figma, etc.) via MCP integrations • *Code Operations* — Read, write, edit, and search code in local repositories • *Custom Agents* — Specialized subagents for tasks like PR review, debugging, incident reports, test writing, and sandbox deployment • *Skills* — Slash-command workflows (`/commit`, `/azure-review`, `/kube-login`, `/azure-login`, `/quip`, etc.) • *Knowledge Notes* — Persistent file-based memory system that retains context across conversations  *Key Workflows:* • *PR Review* — Fetch and review Azure DevOps pull requests with structured feedback • *Jira Integration* — Search, create, update, and comment on Jira issues • *Incident Reports* — Generate IR documents from Jira tickets or Slack threads • *Integration Tests* — Scaffold and write Brunt test suite specs • *Sandbox Deployment* — Deploy service branches to Fynd Platform sandboxes • *Observability* — Query Grafana dashboards, Sentry errors, New Relic metrics, GCP logs, and MongoDB health • *Auto-Authentication* — Automatically re-authenticates Azure DevOps and GCP/Kubernetes when tokens expire  _sent by shadow.ai_

quality_scores.feedback: Response is severely inaccurate—describes a bot with extensive Jira/Azure/Sentry/Grafana/MongoDB/GCP integration that shadow.ai does not have; shadow.ai is a local Claude Code runner with Slack Socket Mode, not an enterprise monitoring/ticketing platform.
